title: White House Says China Stealing AI Technology
summary: The White House says that some companies from China are stealing AI technology from American companies. AI stands for artificial intelligence, which is when computers do smart things like talk or solve problems. The White House says these Chinese companies use a method called 'distilling' to copy AI. They pretend to be many normal users of an AI tool and then try to get secret information from it. The US government wants to help American AI companies protect their technology. A person from China's embassy in the US said that China develops its own technology and works with other countries. The US says it will do four things to stop the stealing, but it did not say exactly what they are. This is important because AI is a big part of our future, and countries want to be the best at it.

keywords.explanation: short for artificial intelligence, when computers can think and learn like people
a way to copy AI technology by pretending to be many users
taking something that does not belong to you without permission
the building where the US President works, also means the US government

questions.question: What method did the White House say Chinese companies use to copy AI?
Who said that China develops its own technology?
How many things did the White House say it will do to stop the stealing?
Did the White House detail specific plans to stop the stealing?

background_read: This article is about the US accusing China of stealing AI technology. AI is important because it helps make computers smarter. Countries compete to have the best AI. The US wants to protect its companies. China says it develops its own tech.
Article_Structure: WHO: The White House and Chinese companies
WHAT: The US says China is stealing AI technology
WHERE: In the US and China
WHY: To protect American AI technology and compete with China

perspectives.description: The US says China is stealing AI technology and wants to stop it.
China says it develops its own technology and the US is being unfair.
